item.title: General Motors recalls over 270,000 cars in US over rearview camera issue
item.description: General Motors is recalling 271,770 вҖӢcars in the вҖҢU.S. over an issue with the вҖӢrearview camera, вҖӢthe U.S. National Highway вҒ Traffic Safety вҖӢAdministration (NHTSA) said on Wednesday.

item.title: Stellantis in advanced talks to develop Opel EV with China's Leapmotor, sources say
item.description: Stellantis is in advanced talks with Leapmotor to jointly develop an Opel-branded electric SUV that вҖӢwould use the Chinese automaker's technology and be produced at Stellantis' Zaragoza plant in Spain, three sources told Reuters.

item.title: Greece expected to announce social media ban for children under 15
item.description: Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos вҖӢMitsotakis is expected вҖҢon Wednesday to announce вҖӢa ban вҖӢon access to social вҒ media for вҖӢchildren under the вҖӢage of 15, according to a вҖӢgovernment briefing.

item.title: Swiss banks to test use cases for Swiss franc stablecoin
item.description: Six Swiss banks have joined forces to вҖӢtest potential use cases вҖҢfor a Swiss franc stablecoin in Switzerland, banking giant UBS вҖӢsaid on Wednesday.

item.title: SK Hynix shares jump 15% after peer Samsung projects blowout earnings
item.description: Shares of South Korean вҖӢchipmaker SK Hynix surged on Wednesday вҖҢafter peer Samsung Electronics forecast market-beating quarterly earnings, fuelling expectations about SK Hynix's results.

item.title: China's Xi urges demand-driven growth in services industry
item.description: China's President Xi Jinping вҖӢhas called for вҖҢa demand-driven approach coupled with reform and вҖӢtechnological empowerment вҖӢto develop the service вҒ industry, the official вҖӢXinhua news agency вҖӢreported on Wednesday.

item.title: Morning Bid: Two weeks to breathe
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: US carmakers accuse EU of blocking supersized pick-up trucks from roads, FT reports
item.description: U.S. carmakers have accused Brussels вҖӢof keeping their largest вҖҢpick-up trucks, including the Ford F-150, вҖӢthe Chevy Silverado вҖӢand the Ram 1500, вҒ off European roads, вҖӢthe Financial Times вҖӢreported on Wednesday.

item.title: IATA chief says jet fuel supply could take months to recover after Hormuz reopening
item.description: The head of a body representing global airlines said on Wednesday that even if Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz it would take months вҖӢfor jet fuel supply to recover given disruptions to Middle East refining вҖҢcapacity.

item.title: Exclusive: TikTok to build a second billion-euro data centre in Finland
item.description: TikTok plans to invest 1 вҖҢbillion euros ($1.16 billion) to build a second data centre in Finland in less than a year as it moves data storage for European users to the continent, company officials said on Wednesday.

item.title: US rejects aluminum tariff relief request from Ford, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. government has so far rejected requests from Ford Motor and other U.S. вҖӢautomakers for relief from aluminum tariffs after fires at вҖҢa Novelis plant created supply bottlenecks for vehicles, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

item.title: CK Hutchison's Panama unit files arbitration against Maersk over ports takeover
item.description: Hong Kong-based conglomerate CK Hutchison's Panama unit has launched an arbitration against Maersk after the takeover of two strategic вҖӢports near the Panama Canal which are at the center of вҖҢa legal battle that has embroiled Beijing and Washington.

item.title: Moody's cuts outlook on Blue Owl fund to negative over surge in redemption requests
item.description: Moody's Ratings on Tuesday cut its outlook on a $36 billion Blue Owl fund to negative from stable, citing redemption requests вҖӢthat were "significantly higher" than peers in the first quarter.

item.title: Investor reactions to Trump agreeing to two-week ceasefire with Iran
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that he had agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, less than two hours before his deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face widespread attacks on its civilian infrastructure.

item.title: China's Sigenergy Technology aims to raise $562 mln in Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Sigenergy Technology Co, a China-based energy storage firm, is aiming вҖӢto raise about HK$4.40 billion ($561.6 million) through an вҖҢinitial public offering in Hong Kong, according to a listing document filed to the exchange on Wednesday.

item.title: Central banks' concern over rising geopolitical tensions surges, survey shows
item.description: Concerns among central banks about geopolitical tensions have surged dramatically this year and вҖҢare now viewed as the top global risk, according to a new survey of central banks managing more than $9.5 trillion in reserves.

item.title: US crude futures fall $12.04 to $100.90/bbl after Trump announces two week ceasefire
item.description: U.S. West Texas Intermediate вҖӢcrude oil fell вҖҢmore than $12 per barrel in early вҖӢtrade on вҖӢWednesday after U.S. President вҒ Donald Trump вҖӢsaid he had вҖӢagreed to a ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: US crude futures fall $4.45 or 3.94% to $108.50/bbl on open
item.description: U.S. West вҖӢTexas Intermediate вҖҢcrude oil fell in вҖӢearly trade вҖӢon Wednesday, roughly вҒ two вҖӢhours ahead of вҖӢU.S. President Donald Trump's deadline вҖӢfor вҖӢIran to open the вҖҢStrait вҒ of Hormuz.

item.title: Chicago jet fuel tops $5 a gallon, as refiner maintenance adds to war-related price surge
item.description: Chicago has become the most expensive U.S. jet fuel market, with prices topping $5 per gallon, as the disruptions caused by the Iran вҖӢwar have combined with previously scheduled refinery maintenance to leave regional supply вҖҢtight.

item.title: US SEC says it filed 456 enforcement actions during fiscal year 2025
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on Tuesday вҖӢthe agency obtained orders for monetary вҖҢrelief totaling $17.9 billion during fiscal year 2025 and that it had filed 456 enforcement вҖӢactions during that period.

item.title: Fed's Jefferson sees risks to both employment, inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson on Tuesday reiterated his view that short-term borrowing costs are set appropriately to allow the central вҖӢbank to respond as needed to the uncertain effects of rising вҖҢenergy prices and the conflict in the Middle East on the Fed's two mandates of price stability and full employment.

item.title: Germany intelligence agency warns of Russian APT28 cyber spying
item.description: GermanyвҖҷs domestic intelligence agency warned on вҖӢTuesday of cyberattacks by the вҖҢRussian state-linked hacker group APT28, saying it had compromised vulnerable TP-Link internet вҖӢrouters to spy on military, вҖӢgovernment and critical infrastructure targets.

item.title: Super Micro begins independent probe after criminal case against coвҖ‘founder, others
item.description: Super Micro said on Tuesday it had begun an independent investigation into the indictment of three people linked to the company вҖӢon the charges of committing export-control violations.

item.title: Trading Day: Trump's Iran deadline looms
item.description: Wall Street recovered earlier losses to end mostly flat on Tuesday, as traders awaited the deadline later in the evening that U.S. President Donald Trump has given Iran to re-open the Strait of Hormuz and get oil and gas supplies flowing again.

item.title: Levi Strauss raises annual forecasts on resilient demand for premium jeans
item.description: Levi вҖӢStrauss on Tuesday raised its annual sales and profit forecasts after beating first-quarter вҖҢestimates, as resilient demand for its premium denims and strength in its direct-to-consumer business helped offset a hit from U.S. tariffs.

item.title: US transportation secretary sees room for airline mergers
item.description: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said on Tuesday he thinks there is вҖӢroom for consolidation in the U.S. airline industry but said вҖҢany potential deal would face close scrutiny on how it would affect consumers.

item.title: PIMCO weighs $14 billion debt deal for Oracle's Michigan data center, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is in talks with the Bank of America to help provide roughly $14 billion of debt вҖӢfinancing to build an Oracle data center in Michigan, вҖҢBloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US short-term credit market shows early signs of stress as Iran war persists
item.description: A prolonged war in the Middle East is starting to ripple into U.S. short-term credit markets, where subtle strains are emerging that could amplify liquidity risks.

item.title: Panama authorizes First Quantum to remove material from closed copper mine
item.description: Panama's trade ministry has issued вҖӢa resolution allowing First Quantum Minerals to вҖҢremove material from its shuttered Cobre Panama mine out of the country.

item.title: Goolsbee says stagflationary shock of Iran war puts Fed in a bind
item.description: Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President вҖӢAustan Goolsbee said on Tuesday he is worried that the Iran war will вҖҢdrive inflation higher even as it slows the U.S. economy, putting the Fed in an uncomfortable position where there is no obvious "cookbook" for what to do.
